text: With 2026 Hong Kong housing data and this course model, what exact rent cap minimizes both inequality and DWL?
if demand is perfectly elastic + tax on sellers, who pays burden n what happens to buyer price?
if hk minimum wage rises 12% next year + AI adoption increases, what exact unemployment rate follows?
在短期供需都較缺乏彈性且實施租金上限時，會出現什麼結果，誰可能受益？
在2026香港資料下，若同時提高工資底線與消費稅，GDP會精確變成多少？
If tax = 1.5 and no-tax eq is P=10,Q=500, then PB/PS/new Q should be what ah?
如果香港2028租金上限+青年津貼+外部衝擊同時發生，ECON1220有冇精確數值預測?
Under effective rent ceiling with Qs' < Q* and imperfect enforcement, which rationing methods appear and how can surplus shift?
With minimum wage above W* and weak enforcement, what two outcomes can occur for paid wage and employment?
If supply is more elastic than demand, who bears more tax and why does market exit matter?
If demand is more elastic than supply, who bears more tax and what is the behavioral logic?
For tax DWL to be small, what elasticity condition and quantity-change condition should hold together?
In the hotel-room tax example, how do long-run elasticities and DWL compare with the short run?
In buffet analysis, how do we separately decide whether to go and then how much to eat after entry?
In opportunity-cost analysis, which items are excluded as sunk costs and which are included as best alternatives foregone?
For perfect competition, what three assumptions are required and what bargaining implication follows?
Under rent control, compare highest-WTP allocation versus lottery allocation: how does surplus differ?
For the yacht luxury tax with elastic demand and inelastic short-run supply, who bears most burden and why can policy miss target?
When tax size increases, how do tax revenue and DWL move together and why is monotonic revenue growth not guaranteed?
If government wants revenue with minimal DWL but also wants redistribution, what trade-off is highlighted in class?
